steps.title: Rearrange and complete the square
steps.content:
Rearrange to collect x and y terms on the left:
$$x^2 + 14x + y^2 - 6y = 109$$
Complete the square for x: Since the coefficient of x is 14, take half and square: $(14/2)^2 = 49$.
$$x^2 + 14x = (x+7)^2 - 49$$
Complete the square for y: Since the coefficient of y is $-6$, take half and square: $(-6/2)^2 = 9$.
$$y^2 - 6y = (y-3)^2 - 9$$

steps.title: Convert to standard form and find the radius
steps.content:
Substitute the completed squares:
$$(x+7)^2 - 49 + (y-3)^2 - 9 = 109$$
Move constants to the right side:
$$(x+7)^2 + (y-3)^2 = 109 + 49 + 9 = 167$$
This matches the standard form for a circle with $r^2 = 167$. Therefore, $r = \sqrt{167}$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
